'''Matthew Russell''' (born 6 June 1993) is a Scottish professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] and [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] at international level.

He previously played for [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League|top flight]]. Russell spent a season with the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. He later played for [[Warrington Wolves]] in the Super League, and spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[2016 RFL Championship|Championship]]. Russell also played for the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the top flight.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Russell was born in [[Irvine, North Ayrshire]], Scotland.{{cn|date=June 2025}}

==Career==
===Wigan Warriors===
Russell's [[Super League]] career started with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], playing some games on loan to [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].

===Gold Coast Titans===
He then agreed a two-year deal with Australian [[NRL]] team, the [[Gold Coast Titans]], but returned to England a season later.

===Warrington Wolves===
On 16 April 2014, the entertaining, fast running Scottish fullback, signed a new three-year deal that would keep him at [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] until November 2017.<ref name="Matty Russell seals new Wolves deal">{{cite web |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/29999/matty-russell-seals-new-wolves |title=Matty Russell seals new Wolves deal |publisher=superleague.co.uk |date=31 December 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140421204123/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/29999/matty-russell-seals-new-wolves |access-date=1 January 2018 |archive-date=2014-04-21}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

===Toronto Wolfpack===
He later signed a two-year deal with Championship club [[Toronto Wolfpack]].<ref name="Wigan reserve fullback Matty Russell has agreed a two year deal with NRL outfit Gold Coast Titans">{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12215/8136452/wigan-reserve-full-back-matty-russell-has-agreed-a-two-year-deal-with-nrl-outfit-gold-coast-titans|title=Wigan reserve fullback Matty Russell has agreed a two year deal with NRL outfit Gold Coast Titans|publisher=skysports.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>
[[File:Featherstone v Toronto.jpg|right|thumb|Russell scoring a try against Featherstone in 2019]]

===Leigh Centurions===
On 9 November 2020 it was announced that Russell would join [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=9 Nov 2020|access-date=25 Dec 2020|title=Matty Russell joins Leigh Centurions|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/57334/matty-russell-joins-leigh-centurions}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 6 November 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 Nov 2021|accessdate=6 Nov 2021|title=Matty Russell makes Super League move|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/matty-russell-makes-super-league-move/}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, Russell scored Toulouse Olympique's first ever try in the competition, however it was not enough as they were defeated 42-14 by [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/toulouse-olympique-wont-be-easy-pickings-insists-giants-wardle-3568479/|title=Toulouse Olympique won't be easy pickings for any one in Super League, says Huddersfield Giants' Jake Wardle|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk |date=15 February 2022 }}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves (rejoin)===
After Toulouse were relegated from the Super League, Russell signed a contract to join Warrington. Russell played 14 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=3 October 2023 }}</ref>

===Hull F.C. (loan)===
On 27 February 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Feb 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Warrington Wolves winger Matty Russell joins Super League rivals on loan|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-winger-matty-russell-joins-super-league-rivals-hull-fc-on-loan}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos (loan)===
On 16 May 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]] on short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=16 May 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Warrington Wolves winger Matty Russell makes loan move to Super League rivals|url=https://www.totalrl.com/warrington-wolves-winger-matty-russell-makes-loan-move-to-super-league-rivals/}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 30 Sep 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=30 Sep 2024|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Wakefield Trinity snap up Warrington Wolves ace ahead of 2025 Super League return|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-trinity-snap-up-warrington-wolves-ace-ahead-of-2025-super-league-return}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 3 December 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=3 Dec 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Wakefield Trinity winger Matty Russell signs for Oldham|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-wakefield-trinity-winger-matty-russell-signs-for-oldham/}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
On 13 February 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]], without playing any games for [[Oldham RLFC]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=14 February 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Matty Russell secures Super League return in immediate transfer|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/matty-russell-secures-super-league-return-in-immediate-transfer/}}</ref>

==International career==
Russell was named in the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="McCormack finalises Scotland Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1128/mccormack-finalises-scotland-squad|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131003131734/http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1128/mccormack-finalises-scotland-squad|url-status=usurped|archive-date=3 October 2013|title=McCormack finalises Scotland Squad|publisher=rlwc2013.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref><ref name="Uncapped Russell named in Scotland's World Cup squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldscotland.com/sport/rugby/uncapped-russell-named-in-scotlands-world-cup-squad.1380539400|title=Uncapped Russell named in Scotland's World Cup squad|publisher=heraldscotland.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref> He was one of the star players in the tournament.

In 2016, Russell was part of Scotland's [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] campaign, where he scored a try in their second game against England. He also played in the historic game against New Zealand, where Scotland's 18-18 draw saw them become the first 'fourth nation' to earn a point since the Four Nations series inaugurated in 2009.<ref name="Scotland make history in Four Nations draw with holders New Zealand">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/nov/11/new-zealand-scotland-four-nations-match-report|title=Scotland make history in Four Nations draw with holders New Zealand|publisher=theguardian.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>
